role_title: Associate Manager, New Verticals - Strategy & Operations

location.office_time_required: a few days a month in person together

level_signals: Comp range $105,400–$155,000 base consistent with mid-senior IC
Associate Manager title typical of mid-level ops roles at DoorDash

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4-6+ years of experience. You've succeeded in similar cultures that are highly analytical and cross-functional (whether in tech, operations, strategy, consulting, banking, or a related experience).
you're comfortable pulling your own data in SQL and building models in excel.
You're analytical. You let data win arguments, and you're comfortable pulling your own data in SQL and building models in excel.
You solve problems from first principles. You have a talent for synthesizing complex problems, can translate high-level goals into actionable plans, and enjoy implementing a process
You are an owner. You're always looking for opportunities to better your work product and own the outcome – both the good and the bad
You're entrepreneurial. You have a bias for action and are able to build things from the ground up.
You're unafraid of ambiguity. Rapid change and learning is exciting to you, and you thrive in ambiguous, startup environment

You're energized by AI tools — and you actively experiment with them. You don't wait to be told how to use a new tool; you've already tried it. You bring a builder's mindset to AI: using it to automate the repetitive, accelerate the analytical, and unlock work that would otherwise require a larger team.
Lead, collaborate and Influence. You'll partner with and influence cross-functional teams including growth, quality, finance, product and engineering to launch initiatives to the finish line.
You have a completed Bachelor's Degree.

scope.surface: New Verticals (Grocery, Convenience, Alcohol, Retail, DashMarts) — operational errors, merchant tooling, dasher performance, consumer experience
scope.business_model: marketplace (B2B2C) — connecting consumers, dashers, and merchants for on-demand delivery of non-restaurant goods
primary_metrics_inferred: Financial outcomes / cost reduction from order errors
Week-over-week operational metrics on tracking dashboards
core_responsibilities: Develop strategy for best serving all sides of the marketplace — dashers, merchants, and consumers
Own tracking dashboards and manage week-over-week metrics
Build models using Excel and SQL to evaluate economic, operational, and strategic costs and benefits
Transform strategies into operational experiments, product features, and net-new tooling
Use AI tools to automate manual processes, build prototypes, and develop product visions
Partner with and influence cross-functional teams to launch initiatives

green_flags: Equity grants offered in addition to base salary
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail (401k match, 16 weeks parental leave, medical/dental/vision, etc.)
Hiring for two roles simultaneously — increases likelihood of offer
Clear and specific product surface area (operational errors in non-restaurant verticals)
Hybrid model is light (a few days a month), effectively near-remote
Role explicitly described as individual contributor — no people management burden
red_flags: No visa sponsorship information stated — unclear for international candidates
Posting first seen / last updated dates unavailable — freshness unclear
No specific cities listed for hybrid office requirement — location obligations ambiguous
4-6+ years range is broad; actual calibration to seniority may vary by team need and interview performance
AI tool usage framed as an expected ongoing shift in scope — role responsibilities may evolve significantly

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, comp range, and IC designation)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and marketplace context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.cities_listed (not named for this role; national pay range cited)
ambiguities: The JD states 'a few days a month in person together' for hybrid but does not name specific office cities — work location requirements are partially unclear.
The pay range is described as 'national base pay range... including Illinois and Colorado' suggesting location-based adjustments apply but the exact mechanism is not specified.
The JD notes candidates will be considered for both Associate Manager roles and aligned based on skills and interview performance — the two roles may have meaningfully different day-to-day scopes.
